microsoft_ft: Served as the **forward-deployed bridge** between enterprise customers (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp) and engineering, translating field integration challenges into platform improvements that drove **$14M+ business impact**.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining self-service scheduling requirements and deployment workflows that cut operational toil by **30%** and scaled execution across 45+ annual drills.

Built an **AI agent** for drill planning that restructured end-to-end workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and expanding drill capacity by 21% — turning a manual process into a scalable, automated system.
Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, acting as integration lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation deployment tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, shipping a full POC in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** and surfacing critical hardware defects that directly informed product roadmap priorities.
microsoft_2023: Championed **developer adoption** of AI-driven code generation tooling, running hands-on demos and integration sessions across 14 Azure service teams that saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.
Identified critical feature gaps by conducting 11+ user interviews with developer teams, translating patterns in customer feedback into a roadmap addition for **intent-based YAML generation**.
Accelerated developer experience by migrating documentation to an AI-powered **semantic search** system with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% across internal engineering users.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and cut region launch times by 28% by aligning 140+ teams on a unified automation prioritization framework — turning fragmented customer feedback into a clear product strategy.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to surface 81 hours of manual effort per region launch, then built a **deployment automation roadmap** that eliminated critical-path toil and unblocked faster customer onboarding.
Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps and prioritize high-impact product investments with real field data.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot by owning the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations and hands-on debugging integration failures in the field.

Implemented automated **CI/CD pipelines** in partnership with IT, Operations, and Engineering, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual intervention at each release stage.
Facilitated Agile ceremonies and owned backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and unblock cross-team adoption of the new platform.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end customer integration lifecycle — building a CRM workflow that managed scoping, proposals, and invoicing for 200+ monthly engagements.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web and search touchpoints to improve the end-user onboarding funnel.
Improved leadership pricing models by analyzing **1,000+ project records** to identify margin leaks, delivering actionable findings that directly shaped product and operational priorities.

skills_priority: Python, APIs, CI/CD, Azure, YAML, SQL
Data Structures, Algorithms, Machine Learning, AI
tailoring_notes: 'Forward-deployed bridge' and 'integration lead' language from the JD woven into microsoft_ft bullets 1 and 4 to mirror Tavus's 30-40% customer-facing split.
'Developer experience', 'semantic search', and 'intent-based' — JD-specific developer tooling vocabulary — surfaced in microsoft_2023 bullets 1, 2, and 3.
'POC' and 'deployment automation roadmap' from the JD's core responsibilities landed in microsoft_ft bullet 5 and microsoft_2022 bullet 2.
'Customer feedback → product roadmap translation' framing (the JD's PM-hat requirement) applied explicitly in microsoft_ft bullet 1 and pro_painters bullet 1.
'Hands-on debugging integration failures' mirrors the JD's 'debug integrations' language and was placed in amazon_robotics bullet 1 for cross-role coverage.
